query: Moderna mRNA-1345 pediatric maternal RSV vaccine clinical trial phase status 2025

mako.raw:
{"query":"Moderna mRNA-1345 pediatric maternal RSV vaccine clinical trial phase status 2025","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1541,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC12439546/","title":"Safety and immunogenicity of an mRNA-based RSV vaccine in seropositive children aged 12–59 months - PMC","content":"by SS Ghamloush · 2025 · Cited by 8 — In the pivotal phase 3 trial in adults ≥60 years, mRNA-1345 demonstrated a vaccine efficacy of 83.7% and 82.4% against RSV-LRTD with ≥2 and ≥3 ...Read more
CONTACT Matthew Snape Matthew.Snape@modernatx.com Moderna Biotech Manufacturing UK, Harwell Science and Innovation Centre, Harwell Campus, Rutherford Ave, Didcot OX11 0GD, United Kingdom.
This phase 1, randomized, observer-blind, placebo-controlled, dose-ranging study evaluated the safety, reactogenicity, and immunogenicity of three injections of mRNA-1345 at two dose levels (15 μg or 30 μg) in RSV-seropositive children aged 12–59 months across 15 centers in the United States from June 2021 to March 2024 (ClinicalTrials.gov, NCT04528719).
Comparatively, among placebo recipients, respective Month 1 RSV-A and RSV-B nAb GMFRs (95% CIs) from baseline were 1.4 (0.7–3.2) and 1.2 (0.6–2.8).
These findings supported further clinical investigation of mRNA-1345 in pediatric populations.
He received his MBBS and MD degrees from the University of Melbourne, and is based at the Moderna Innovation and Technology Centre, Harwell, UK.
Such requests can be made to Moderna, Inc., 325 Binney Street, Cambridge, MA 02142; or data_sharing@modernatx.com.
Novel drug approvals for 2025.
Safety and immunogenicity of an mRNA-based RSV vaccine in Japanese older adults aged ≥60 years: a phase 1, randomized, observer-blind, placebo-controlled trial."},{"url":"https://www.vaccineadvisor.com/news/moderna-seeks-approval-of-mrna-based-rsv-vaccine-for-older-adults/","title":"Moderna Seeks Approval of mRNA-Based RSV Vaccine for Older Adults - Vaccine Advisor","content":"Moderna has initiated a rolling submission of a Biologics License Application (BLA) for mRNA-1345, an investigational respiratory syncytial virus (RSV) vaccine for the prevention of RSV-associated lower respiratory tract disease (RSV-LRTD) and acute respiratory disease in adults aged 60 years or older.
The BLA is supported by data from the double-blind, placebo-controlled ConquerRSV trial (ClinicalTrials.gov Identifier: NCT05127434).
The study enrolled approximately 37,000 adults 60 years of age and older.
Vaccine efficacy against RSV-LRTD with 3 or more symptoms was reported to be 82.4% (96.36% CI, 34.8-95.3; P =.0078).
A phase 1 trial evaluating mRNA-1345 in pediatric patients (ClinicalTrials.gov Identifier: NCT05743881) has also been initiated.
Moderna announces mRNA-1345, an investigational respiratory syncytial virus (RSV) vaccine, has met primary efficacy endpoints in phase 3 trial in older adults."},{"url":"https://ctv.veeva.com/study/a-study-of-mrna-1345-an-mrna-vaccine-targeting-respiratory-syncytial-virus-in-children-2-to-18-ye","title":"A Study of mRNA-1345, an mRNA Vaccine Targeting Respiratory Syncytial Virus, in Children 2 to <18 Years of Age at High Risk of Respiratory Syncytial Virus","content":"Jul 10, 2025 — Part A: The purpose is to evaluate the safety, reactogenicity, and immunogenicity of mRNA-1345 in children aged 2 to <5 years (Cohort 1) and ...Read more
Part A: The purpose is to evaluate the safety, reactogenicity, and immunogenicity of mRNA-1345 in children aged 2 to <5 years (Cohort 1) and in children at high risk of respiratory syncytial virus (RSV) disease 5 to <18 years of age (Cohort 2) to inform the dose level selection for the next phase of development (Phase 3).
Healthy, or with stable chronic conditions increasing the risk of RSV disease, per the clinical judgment of the Investigator."},{"url":"https://www.fda.gov/media/184378/download","title":"Moderna Final","content":"Dec 12, 2024 — Review of Investigational RSV (mRNA-1345) y demonstrated in global study of >36,000 adults; RSV (mRNA-1345) Data cut-off 15 October 2024."}]}
